prevalence.source: Dementia epidemiology syntheses; Brazilian aging studies
prevalence.trend: increasing with aging and vascular risk factors

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to disability and caregiver burden
burden.mortalityRate: Moderate (driven by comorbidity and stroke risk)

vulnerablePopulations: low-income populations with poor vascular risk control
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Cognitive decline with vascular events or imaging evidence; neuroimaging (CT/MRI) supports diagnosis; exclude other dementias

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: opportunistic cognitive screening in primary care
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; depends on vascular risk management and support services

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical assessment with imaging in referral centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Vascular risk factor control, antiplatelet therapy when indicated, cognitive rehabilitation and caregiver support
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to neuroimaging and dementia support services in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Primary care programs for hypertension/diabetes support prevention
